description: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 3 May 2021

title: mRESVIA RSV vaccine approved to protect patients aged 60 and over
description: A study found that around 4 months after vaccination, people who received the RSV vaccine had a 79% reduction in their risk of getting lower respiratory tract disease caused by RSV, compared with those who received placebo.

title: Givinostat conditionally approved to treat patients with Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD)
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has today (20 December 2024) granted a conditional marketing authorisation for the medicine givinostat (Duvyzat) to treat Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD).

title: Applications invited for the role of Chair of the MHRA
description: Ministers are seeking to appoint a Chair of the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA).

title: Warning statements for labels and leaflets of certain medicines
description: This guidance sets out the warning statements which should appear on the label and/or in the leaflet of certain medicines.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: ADVANZ PHARMA, MacroBID 100mg Prolonged-Release Capsules, EL (22)A/50
description: ADVANZ PHARMA has made the MHRA aware that certain batches of MacroBID 100mg Prolonged-Release Capsules have been packed with the incorrect Patient Information Leaflet (PIL).

title: Dapagliflozin (Forxiga): no longer authorised for treatment of type 1 diabetes mellitus
description: The authorisation holder for dapagliflozin has withdrawn the indication for type 1 diabetes mellitus. The removal of the type 1 diabetes indication is not due to any new safety concerns and the other indications of dapagliflozin are unchanged.

title: MHRA showcases next phase of regulatory science to bring innovative treatments to patients sooner
description: Seven new CERSIs came together to showcase how partnerships will modernise regulation in AI, clinical trials, and advanced therapies, bringing innovations to patients sooner.

title: SGLT2 inhibitors: updated advice on the risk of diabetic ketoacidosis
description: Test for raised ketones in patients with ketoacidosis symptoms, even if plasma glucose levels are near-normal.

title: Tredaptive (combined niacin-laropiprant): no longer for prescribing
description: Preliminary HPS2-THRIVE trial failed to show benefit outweighs risks

title: Opdualag licensed for patients with advanced melanoma
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has today (27 December) approved the advanced melanoma medicine Opdualag (nivolumab-relatlimab) for patients from the age of 12.

title: Oral diclofenac no longer available without prescription
description: Oral diclofenac is associated with a small increased risk of cardiovascular side effects and is therefore no longer available over the counter.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Kent Pharma UK, Parasolve (Paracetamol) 500mg effervescent tablets, EL(24)A/51
description: Kent Pharma UK has identified an error in the Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) for Paracetamol 500mg Effervescent Tablets.

title: Statement on Statins
description: Statement from Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency on the risks and benefits of statins.

title: SGLT2 inhibitors (canagliflozin, dapagliflozin, empagliflozin): risk of diabetic ketoacidosis
description: Test for raised ketones in patients with acidosis symptoms, even if plasma glucose levels are near-normal.

title: e-cigarettes and refill containers (e-liquids): report suspected side effects and safety concerns
description: Use the Yellow Card Reporting Scheme to support the safety of e-cigarettes and refill containers (e-liquids).

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 20 December 2021
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 20 December 2021

title: Sir David Spiegelhalter: Regulation in an age of personalised medicine
description: The internationally renowned expert in the calculation and communication of risk and statistics, Sir David Spiegelhalter, discusses the concept of a ‘preference zone’ to frame the demands of safety, efficacy and choice in the era of personalised medicine.

title: Cox-2 selective inhibitors and non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs’ (NSAIDs): Cardiovascular safety
description: Guidance on the cardiovascular safety of Cox-2 inhibitors and NSAIDs and further reading.

title: COVID-19 vaccines and medicines: updates for March 2023
description: Recent information relating to COVID-19 vaccines and medicines that has been published since the February 2023 issue of Drug Safety Update, up to 21 March 2023.

title: Isotretinoin (Roaccutane▼): new safety measures to be introduced in the coming months, including additional oversight on initiation of treatment for patients under 18 years
description: The Isotretinoin Expert Working Group of the Commission on Human Medicines has made recommendations to strengthen the safety of isotretinoin treatment. Recommendations include new warnings, the need for consistent monitoring requirements for psychiatric side effects, the introduction of new monitoring requirements for sexual side effects, and additional oversight of the initiation of treatment for patients younger than 18 years.

title: Winners of global AI cardio-health challenge announced
description:
The successful five winners of a brand new challenge from a collaboration of regulators and healthcare organisations in the UK and US have been announced today (10 January 2024).

The challenge saw 25 teams of artificial intelligence and machine learning developers from across the globe compete against each other to build models that predicted heart failure-related health outcomes for veterans.

title: Regulatory approval of COVID-19 Vaccine SKYCovion
description: Information for healthcare professionals and the public about the COVID-19 Vaccine SKYCovion

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Ethigen Limited, Briviact 75mg & 100mg film-coated tablets, EL(23)A/13
description: Ethigen Limited have informed the MHRA that the Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) in Briviact 75mg and Briviact 100mg film coated tablets contains incorrect or missing information.

title: Dimethyl fumarate (Tecfidera): fatal PML in an MS patient with severe, prolonged lymphopenia
description: Check full blood counts before prescribing dimethyl fumarate and then every 6 to 12 months. Stop treatment immediately if you suspect progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Slenyto 1 mg and 5mg prolonged-release tablets – distributed by Flynn Pharma Ltd, EL (21)A/29
description: Flynn Pharma Ltd, distributors of Slenyto 1 mg and 5mg prolonged release tablets would like to notify you of an error with the patient information leaflets (PILs) that have been packaged in the below batches of products.

title: GLP-1 and dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonists: potential risk of pulmonary aspiration during general anaesthesia or deep sedation
description: Healthcare professionals should be aware of the potential risk of pulmonary aspiration in patients using GLP-1 or dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonists who undergo surgery or procedures with general anaesthesia or deep sedation.

title: Proposal to simplify the information requirements for advertising of medicinal products to prescribers and suppliers of medicines (MLX384)
description: MHRA is asking for feedback on a proposal to simplify the information requirements for advertisements aimed at prescribers and suppliers of medicines.

title: First-ever MHRA analysis of UK clinical trial applications finds new opportunities to drive medical breakthroughs for patients
description: New analysis of the current clinical trial landscape in the UK shows clear opportunities to shape the future of medical research and patient care.

title: Chloramphenicol eye drops containing borax and boric acid buffers: review of the use in children under 2 years
description: Public Assessment Report of our review of the use of chloramphenicol eye drops containing borax and boric acid buffers in children under 2 years.

title: 50th Promising Innovative Medicine (PIM) designation granted
description: As part of the Early Access to Medicines Scheme (EAMS), we have now reached a new PIM designation milestone.

title: Coronavirus: new website for reporting medicines side effects and equipment incidents
description: A new online reporting site, dedicated to reporting any suspected side effects from medicines, future vaccines and incidents involving medical equipment relating to COVID-19 treatment, has today been launched.

title: Classification of Ibuprofen Seven Plus 200mg/5ml Oral Suspension
description: MHRA is asking for feedback on proposals to change the legal classification of Ibuprofen Seven Plus 200mg/5ml Oral Suspension

title: Isotretinoin (Roaccutane▼): introduction of new safety measures, including additional oversight of the initiation of treatment for patients under 18 years of age
description: We have strengthened the safe use of isotretinoin through the introduction of additional oversight of the initiation of isotretinoin in patients under 18 years and through improved assessment and monitoring of mental health and sexual function issues. We ask healthcare professionals to review these new measures and supporting materials and integrate them into their clinical practice when referring patients and when prescribing or dispensing isotretinoin.

title: Valproate (Belvo, Convulex, Depakote, Dyzantil, Epilim, Epilim Chrono or Chronosphere, Episenta, Epival, and Syonell▼): new safety and educational materials to support regulatory measures in men and women under 55 years of age
description: New safety and educational materials have been introduced for men and women and healthcare professionals to reduce the harms from valproate, including the significant risk of serious harm to the baby if taken during pregnancy and the risk of impaired fertility in males.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 26 September 2022
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 26 September 2022.

title: Regulation 251 of the Human Medicines Regulations 2020
description: Confirmation of the regulation 251 of the Human Medicines Regulation following the Brexit transition.

title: Valproate (Epilim▼, Depakote▼) pregnancy prevention programme: updated educational materials
description: In January 2020, healthcare professionals received updated educational materials to support the valproate pregnancy prevention programme. Valproate is contraindicated in girls and women of childbearing potential, unless the conditions of the pregnancy prevention programme are met.

title: Baricitinib (Olumiant▼): increased risk of diverticulitis, particularly in patients with risk factors
description: Use baricitinib with caution in patients with diverticular disease and in those concomitantly treated with medications associated with an increased risk of diverticulitis.

title: Contact lenses recall: 1-day Acuvue Moist for Astigmatism - MHRA urges patients to check their boxes for recalled lots
description: Users of certain Johnson & Johnson Acuvue daily disposable contact lenses are being urged by the MHRA to check their lenses, due to a voluntary product recall of specific lots initiated by the company.

title: Life-saving adrenaline auto-injectors could become available in public places
description: The chance for adults and children suffering from life-threatening allergic reactions to get life-saving shots of adrenaline in public places, such as restaurants and entertainment venues, has come a step closer as a result of a report published today by the government’s independent advisory body, the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM).

title: Isotretinoin (Roaccutane): rare reports of erectile dysfunction and decreased libido
description: Cases of sexual dysfunction, predominantly involving erectile dysfunction and decreased libido, have been reported rarely in patients taking oral isotretinoin for severe acne.

title: Finasteride: reminder of the risk psychiatric side effects and of sexual side effects (which may persist after discontinuation of treatment)
description: A patient alert card is being introduced for men taking finasteride to help raise awareness of the risk of psychiatric side effects and sexual dysfunction, including the potential for sexual dysfunction to persist after treatment has stopped. Healthcare professionals are reminded to monitor patients for both psychiatric and sexual side effects.

title: Early access to medicines scheme applications: pending, refused, granted
description: The number of applications pending, refused and granted for the early access to medicines scheme (EAMS).

title: Angiotensin II receptor antagonists: evidence does not suggest any link with cancer
description: Evidence does not support any increased risk of cancer in patients who use these medicines.

title: Avoid risk of high blood glucose: insert pre-filled insulin cartridges correctly into insulin pump to manage your diabetes
description: People with diabetes using a particular type of insulin pump and pre-filled insulin cartridges should insert their cartridges correctly to avoid them leaking and potentially giving an under-delivery of insulin.

title: MHRA draft guidance on the licensing of biosimilar products
description: We want your comments on the clarity and wording of our new guidance

title: UK regulator confirms that people should continue to receive the COVID-19 vaccine AstraZeneca
description: Following suspensions by some countries of the COVID-19 Vaccine AstraZeneca over suspected blood clots, the MHRA confirms that the benefits of the vaccine in preventing COVID-19 far outweigh the risks. People should still go and get their COVID-19 vaccine when asked to do so.

title: Class 4 Medicines Notification: Argenx BV, Vyvgart 1000 mg solution for injection, EL (24)A/63
description: Argenx BV have informed MHRA that the Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) in the affected packs incorrectly contains reference to two subcutaneous injection sites. Only subcutaneous injection to the abdomen is permitted.

title: MHRA Performance Data
description: Performance data for MHRA key performance indicators (KPIs) and information on the expected timeline to complete a specific regulatory function.

title: MHRA approves Siiltibcy as a diagnostic aid for Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection, including disease
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has approved Siiltibcy

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: CNX Therapeutics Ltd, Latuda film-coated tablets, EL (22)A/13
description: CNX Therapeutics Ltd. have notified the MHRA that the batches of Latuda film-coated tablets listed in this notification will be released without EAN barcodes on the cartons.

title: Esmya (ulipristal acetate): suspension of the licence due to risk of serious liver injury
description: Contact patients currently taking Esmya for uterine fibroids as soon as possible and advise them to stop their treatment. The licence for Esmya has been suspended to protect public health while a safety review is conducted following a further case of liver injury requiring transplant.

title: COVID-19 antivirals: reporting to the UK COVID-19 Antivirals Pregnancy Registry
description: As the safety of COVID-19 antivirals in pregnancy has not been established, please report any pregnancies which occur during use of an antiviral, including paternal use, to the UK COVID-19 Antivirals Pregnancy Registry. This advice applies to molnupiravir (Lagevrio▼), the combination of PF-07321332 (nirmatrelvir) plus ritonavir (Paxlovid▼), and remdesivir (Veklury▼).

title: BIA and MHRA publish report following joint conference Pathway of Innovation from Research to Patients
description: UK BioIndustry Association (BIA) and the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) report following their fifth joint annual conference.

title: Reclassify Soleve Sunburn Relief cutaneous emulsion
description: MHRA is asking for feedback on proposals to reclassify Soleve Sunburn Relief Cutaneous Emulsion from pharmacy to general sales list.

title: Class 3 Medicines Recall: Bristol-Myers Squibb Pharmaceuticals Limited, OPDIVO 10 mg/mL concentrate for solution for infusion (nivolumab), EL(24)A/11
description: Bristol-Myers Squibb Pharmaceuticals Limited has informed the MHRA that a potential product quality issue has been detected, relating to incomplete crimping of the metal crimp cap of OPDIVO 10mg/mL concentrate for solution for infusion (nivolumab) (1VLX10ML).

title: Metformin in pregnancy: study shows no safety concerns
description: A large study has shown no safety issues of concern relating to the use of metformin during pregnancy. The licence for metformin now reflects that it can be considered for use during pregnancy and the periconceptional phase as an addition or an alternative to insulin, if clinically needed.  This is consistent with current clinical guidance.

title: UK medicines regulator joins up with Australia, Canada, Singapore and Switzerland regulators
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) is the latest regulator to join the Australia-Canada-Singapore-Switzerland (ACSS) Consortium.

title: Ulipristal acetate 5mg (Esmya): further restrictions due to risk of serious liver injury
description: The indication of ulipristal acetate 5mg for uterine fibroids has been further restricted due to the risk of serious liver injury and liver failure, with some cases requiring liver transplantation. Although the temporary suspension has been lifted, this medicine should only be used for intermittent treatment of moderate to severe symptoms of uterine fibroids before menopause and when surgical procedures (including uterine fibroid embolisation) are not suitable or have failed.

title: Nicorandil (Ikorel): now second-line treatment for angina - risk of ulcer complications
description: Updated advice on use of nicorandil as second-line treatment for stable angina - some ulcers may progress to complications unless treatment is stopped.

title: Joint MHRA and DIA training: Excellence in Pharmacovigilance
description: 1 to 5 February 2016: This 5-day course will cover European clinical pre and post-marketing safety regulatory requirements.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Rosemont Pharmaceuticals Ltd, Atorvastatin 4mg/ml Oral Suspension & Sildenafil 10mg/ml Oral Suspension , EL (22)A/39
description: Rosemont Pharmaceuticals Ltd. has made the MHRA aware that the expiry dates stamped on the base of the bottle are incorrect for 2 batches of Atorvastatin 4mg/ml Oral Suspension and 2 batches of Sildenafil 10mg/ml Oral Suspension.

title: SGLT2 inhibitors: monitor ketones in blood during treatment interruption for surgical procedures or acute serious medical illness
description: SGLT2 inhibitor treatment should be interrupted in patients who are hospitalised for major surgical procedures or acute serious medical illnesses and ketone levels measured, preferably in blood rather than urine. Treatment may be restarted when the ketone values are normal and the patient's condition has stabilised.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 28 June 2021
description: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 28 June 2021

title: Batches of Ventolin Accuhaler and Seretide Accuhaler asthma inhalers recalled
description: Three specific lots of asthma inhalers are being recalled, including preventer and emergency relief inhalers.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 19 April
description: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 19 April 2021

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Memantine 10mg Film-Coated Tablets, PL 20416/0260, (EL (20)A/11)
description: Crescent Pharma Ltd has informed us of a discrepancy on the product packaging.

title: MHRA Strategy for Improving Safety Communications
description: The MHRA’s strategy for Improving Safety Communications for 2024 to 2027.

title: Letters and drug alerts sent to healthcare professionals in September 2019
description: Letters were issued about parenteral nutrition products, ranibizumab, ingenol mebutate gel, adrenaline auto-injectors, and fingolimod. Recall alerts were recently issued about ranitidine, aripiprazole, and bisacodyl.

title: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency business plan published
description: The 2015/16 business plan for the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency has been published today (30 June 2015).

title: Dame June Raine: How innovations are transforming regulation and speeding new treatments to healthcare
description: As Dame June Raine gets ready to pass the baton on after nearly 40 years at the agency, the last five of which she has been CEO, she reflects on how new innovations are transforming regulation and how honoured she feels to have worked with such inspiring people through a period she has not just lived through but helped to shape.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Strandhaven Ltd t/a Somex Pharma, Clarithromycin 250mg and 500mg film-coated tablets, EL (23)A/42
description: Strandhaven Limited t/a Somex Pharma has informed the MHRA regarding an error with the Patient Information Leaflets (PILs) that have been packed in the listed batches of Clarithromycin 250mg and 500mg film-coated tablets.

title: MHRA publishes final Business Plan for 2023-2026 Corporate Plan
description: The new Business Plan sets out priorities for 2025–26: Protecting public safety and maintaining public trust; delivering efficient, predictable services through regulatory excellence; being an agile organisation that drives innovation; being a great place to work and providing excellent customer service.

title: Codeine-containing pain relief in children
description: Safety review initiated following post-surgical fatalities in ultra-rapid metabolisers

title: MHRA to streamline clinical trial approvals in biggest overhaul of trial regulation in 20 years
description: The legislative changes will help to make the UK one of the best countries in the world to conduct clinical research for patients and researchers.

title: New MHRA Inspectorate blog
description: MHRA has today published its first blog.

title: Orlistat safety update
description: A recent Europe-wide review of the safety information for alli has led to a number of updates, which bring the product information in line with that for Xenical.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 24 May 2021
description: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 24 May 2021

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information, Sevredol 10 mg and 20mg  tablets, (PL 16950/0063, PL 16950/0064), EL (21)A/17
description: Napp Pharmaceuticals Limited have noticed and an error in the PIL of specific batches of  Sevredol 10 mg Tablets and Sevredol 20 mg Tablets.

title: New chief executive appointed at MHRA
description: Lawrence Tallon is appointed as the new Chief Executive Officer of Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA).

title: Pioglitazone: risk of bladder cancer
description: Use of pioglitazone is associated with a small increased risk of bladder cancer. Healthcare professionals should be aware of new warnings and precautions for use in at-risk patients

title: Professor Claire Stewart  appointed to  the Commission on Human Medicines as three members reappointed
description: The appointment will involve a time commitment of 22 days per year. Remuneration for the role will be at a rate of £325 per attendance and preparation for meetings.

title: MHRA advises men taking valproate and their partners to use effective contraception
description: Men taking valproate and their partners are being advised by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) to use effective contraception because of new data suggesting a potential small increased risk of harm to children if valproate is used by a father at conception. Valproate is licensed for epilepsy and bipolar disorder.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 16 May 2022
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 16 May 2022

title: Agile, responsive regulation helps early breast cancer patients to benefit from a study to potentially improve survival outcomes
description: Following MHRA approval of a clinical trial, early breast cancer patients on endocrine-based therapy can benefit from a study with camizestrant.

title: Irbesartan 300mg film-coated tablets - blisters is incorrectly labelled as Irbesartan 150mg
description: (Accord Healthcare Limited) Recipients should quarantine any remaining stock and return it to supplier for credit - class 2 action within 48 hours. (EL (13)A/32)

title: Availability of diclofenac as a pharmacy medicine (MLX 382)
description: The MHRA is undertaking a public consultation on the continued availability of diclofenac as a Pharmacy (P) medicine.

title: MHRA seizes more than 100 unreliable HIV home-testing kits
description: MHRA are investigating a number of Hightop HIV/AIDS Home Test Kits that may be unreliable and may provide false results.

title: Monitoring the safety of antiretroviral drugs
description: Healthcare professionals should monitor the use and safety of antiretroviral drugs for the treatment of HIV as they are advanced.

title: MHRA launches new fees calculator
description: New aid launched to help determine the fee that will be charged upon the submission of an application to the MHRA.

title: Regulator issues warning over small number of TRUEyou home-use blood glucose test strips
description: TRUEyou blood glucose test strips - certain lots of test strips may give incorrect low blood glucose results that could lead to undetected hyperglycaemia.

title: GLP-1 receptor agonists: reports of diabetic ketoacidosis when concomitant insulin was rapidly reduced or discontinued
description: Diabetic ketoacidosis has been reported in patients with type 2 diabetes on a combination of a GLP-1 receptor agonist and insulin who had doses of concomitant insulin rapidly reduced or discontinued. GLP-1 receptor agonists are not substitutes for insulin, and any reduction of insulin should be done in a stepwise manner with careful glucose self-monitoring. Abrupt discontinuation or reduction in insulin doses can lead to poor glycaemic control, with a risk of diabetic ketoacidosis.

title: Improving collaboration to protect public health – MHRA and Care Quality Commission sign MoU
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) and Care Quality Commission (CQC) set out key principles to support their collaboration on improving public health.

title: Regulator issues urgent warning over defective hearing aid batteries
description: People who have bought ZeniPower mercury-free hearing aid batteries to check the best before date on the packs due to two defective batches.

title: Warfarin and other anticoagulants – monitoring of patients during the COVID-19 pandemic
description: The MHRA has published advice for patients and healthcare professionals

title: Topical testosterone (Testogel): risk of harm to children following accidental exposure
description: Premature puberty and genital enlargement have been reported in children who were in close physical contact with an adult using topical testosterone and who were repeatedly accidentally exposed to this medicine. To reduce these risks, advise patients to wash their hands after application of topical testosterone, cover the application site with clothing once the product has dried, and wash the application site before physical contact with another adult or child.

title: Herbal medicines granted a traditional herbal registration (THR)
description: A list of herbal medicines currently holding a traditional herbal registration (THR) granted by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA).

title: Onsenal▼ (Celecoxib) withdrawn from EU market
description: Onsenal▼ (celecoxib) is no longer approved in Europe for the reduction of intestinal polyps in familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP).

title: Ivacaftor, tezacaftor, elexacaftor (Kaftrio▼) in combination with ivacaftor (Kalydeco): risk of serious liver injury; updated advice on liver function testing
description: Cases of serious liver injury with elevated transaminases and bilirubin have been reported during treatment with Kaftrio–Kalydeco combination therapy. In all patients, measure alanine aminotransferase (ALT), aspartate aminotransferase (AST), and total bilirubin levels before starting treatment, every 3 months during the first year of treatment, and annually thereafter.

title: Carfilzomib (Kyprolis▼): risk of reactivation of hepatitis B virus
description: Establish hepatitis B status before initiating carfilzomib and in patients with unknown hepatitis B virus serology who are already being treated with carfilzomib.

title: Fluoroquinolone antibiotics: new restrictions and precautions for use due to very rare reports of disabling and potentially long-lasting or irreversible side effects
description: Disabling, long-lasting or potentially irreversible adverse reactions affecting musculoskeletal and nervous systems have been reported very rarely with fluoroquinolone antibiotics. Fluoroquinolone treatment should be discontinued at the first signs of a serious adverse reaction, including tendon pain or inflammation.

title: Telbivudine and pegylated interferon: risk of peripheral neuropathy
description: Patients who take telbivudine and pegylated interferon are at increased risk of peripheral neuropathy and should stop taking both medicines if symptoms occur.

title: Professor Sir Michael Rawlins re-appointed Chair
description: Professor Sir Michael will return as Chair for a second term.

title: Moderna COVID-19 vaccine approved by MHRA in 12-17 year olds
description: The UK regulator has confirmed the vaccine is safe and effective in this age group.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 21 June 2021
description: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 21 June 2021

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Aspen Pharma Trading Limited, Co-trimoxazole 80mg/400mg per 5ml adult suspension, EL (24)A/32
description: Aspen Pharma Trading Limited has informed the MHRA that an error has been found in the Patient Information Leaflet.

title: New streamlined notification scheme for lowest-risk clinical trials marks start of MHRA overhaul of regulation
description: The scheme will see the lowest-risk clinical trials processed by the MHRA in less than 14 days.

title: Febuxostat (Adenuric): increased risk of cardiovascular death and all-cause mortality in clinical trial in patients with a history of major cardiovascular disease
description: Avoid treatment with febuxostat in patients with pre-existing major cardiovascular disease (for example, myocardial infarction, stroke, or unstable angina), unless no other therapy options are appropriate. Findings from a phase 4 clinical study (the CARES study) in patients with gout and a history of major cardiovascular disease show a higher risk for cardiovascular-related death and for all-cause mortality in patients assigned to febuxostat than in those assigned to allopurinol.

title: The Human Medicines (Amendment etc.) (EU Exit) (No. 2) and the Medical Devices (Amendment etc.) (EU Exit) (No. 2) Regulations 2019
description: These Regulations (which come into force immediately before exit day) are made to correct drafting defects and omissions in the Human Medicines (Amendment etc.) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019 (S.I. 2019/775) and the Medical Devices (Amendment etc.) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019 (S.I. 2019/791). This is to ensure that the published policy in relation to the regulation of human medicines and medical devices in a no deal EU exit scenario is properly reflected in those instruments. Those instruments, which come into force on exit day, amend the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 (S.I. 2012/1916 – “HMRs”), associated Medicines (Fees) Regulations 2016 and the Medical Devices Regulations 2002 (S.I. 2002/618 – “MDRs”) to ensure they are fit for purpose in a no deal EU exit.

title: Medicines and Healthcare Products Regulatory Agency: annual report and accounts 2013 to 2014
description: Annual report and accounts for the Medicines and Healthcare Products Regulatory Agency for 2013 to 2014.

title: Strengthening collaboration between the MHRA and the Department of Health Northern Ireland
description: The MHRA and Northern Ireland partners are working together to support innovation, enhance patient safety and ensure people in Northern Ireland benefit from world-class regulation.

title: Direct-acting antivirals to treat chronic hepatitis C: risk of interaction with vitamin K antagonists and changes in INR
description: INR should be monitored closely during treatment of chronic hepatitis C with direct-acting antivirals in patients also receiving vitamin K antagonists (eg, warfarin), because of possible changes in liver function during treatment.

title: Ground-breaking WEB-RADR project marks mid-point
description: The MHRA-led WEB-RADR project held its General Assembly in London on April 21

title: Dr Janet Valentine appointed as new CPRD Director
description: MHRA announces the appointment of new Clinical Practice Research Datalink (CPRD) director Dr Janet Valentine with effect from today.

title: BIA and MHRA publish report ‘Collaborative Working in the UK, Driving Innovation Forward’ following their eighth annual joint conference
description: The UK BioIndustry Association (BIA) and the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) brought together experts from across the life sciences sector to discuss some hot topics and important developments through a series of presentations and panel discussions.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Notification: Renacet 475 mg and 950 mg Tablets (calcium acetate), RenaCare NephroMed GmbH, EL(25)A15
description: RenaCare NephroMed GmbH has informed the MHRA of the presence of an undeclared excipient in the coating of the tablets. This excipient is Macrogol 6000, which has always been included in Renacet Tablets but has been omitted from the list of excipients in error.

title: UK and Singapore launch a regulatory innovation corridor to speed up access to breakthrough health technologies
description: Patients in the UK and Singapore could gain faster access to cutting-edge healthcare innovations under a new partnership bringing two globally respected regulators together with one of the world’s leading biotech creators.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 8 November 2021
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 8 November 2021

title: Methotrexate: advise patients to take precautions in the sun to avoid photosensitivity reactions
description: Photosensitivity reactions are known side effects of methotrexate treatment and can be severe. Patients should be advised to take precautions to protect their skin in the sun.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 10 October 2022
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 10 October 2022.

title: Regulator warns against purchasing inaccurate infrared thermometers
description: The public and healthcare professionals are being warned to take care when purchasing infrared thermometers online and to familiarise themselves with warning signs for poor quality products.

title: Dimethyl fumarate (Tecfidera): updated advice on the risk of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) associated with mild lymphopenia
description: The monitoring requirements and discontinuation criteria for dimethyl fumarate (Tecfidera) have been strengthened following a small number of reports of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML) in patients with mild lymphopenia. Continue to monitor lymphocyte counts and advise patients to seek urgent medical attention if they experience any symptoms or signs suggestive of PML.

title: Don’t rely on temperature screening products for detection of coronavirus (COVID-19), says MHRA
description: Warning that thermal cameras and other such “temperature screening” products, some of which make direct claims to screen for COVID-19, are not a reliable way to detect if people have the virus.

title: Recognised safety profile of flu antivirals and vaccines confirmed
description: No unexpected new safety issues have been identified from Yellow Card reports.

title: Flucytosine (Ancotil): new contraindication in patients with DPD deficiency
description: Flucytosine is a prodrug of 5-fluorouracil used to treat systemic yeast and fungal infections and can cause life-threatening and severe toxicity in patients with complete and partial dihydropyrimidine dehydrogenase (DPD) deficiency. Although pre-testing of DPD status before flucytosine treatment is not required, a new contraindication for patients with complete DPD deficiency has been introduced.

title: Viread 245 mg film-coated tablets - possible presence of silicone rubber
description: (Gilead Sciences Limited) Pharmacists should quarantine any remaining stocks affected batches and return them to the original wholesaler for credi - class 2 action within 48 hours. (EL (14)A/03)

title: Daclizumab (Zinbryta▼) and risk of severe liver injury: initiation in multiple sclerosis now restricted, promptly review patients already on treatment
description: While an urgent EU-wide review of new information on liver safety is under way, promptly review patients on treatment. Only initiate daclizumab in restricted groups of patients with limited treatment options and keep all patients under close liver function monitoring.

title: MHRA guidance on new Medical Devices Post-Market Surveillance requirements
description: A suite of guidance has been published by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA).

title: Poster from the Independent Medicines and Medical Devices Safety Review
description: The Independent Medicines and Medical Devices Safety Review (IMMDS) has asked the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency to place some information on its website. The IMMDS has produced a poster to make sure people are aware of its work and have the opportunity to make contact or provide evidence on 3 medical interventions.

title: All types of metal-on-metal hip implants need regular review
description: MHRA updates guidance for healthcare professionals.

title: Ordering HIV self-test kits online? Make sure you get the right kit
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) is reminding people to be careful when ordering HIV self-test kits online.

title: Innovation: Queen’s University Belfast
description: MHRA helps Queen’s University Belfast bring innovation and regulation together

title: MHRA introduces new restrictions for fluoroquinolone antibiotics
description: Following a review of the effectiveness of current measures to reduce the risk of potentially long-term or irreversible side effects associated with fluoroquinolone antibiotics, the MHRA has introduced further restrictions to limit their use.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 12 April 2021
description: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 12 April 2021

title: Dodgy dental equipment could come back to bite you
description: The internet provides easy access to a wide and diverse range of medical products and there has been rapid growth in sites offering cheaper alternatives but some of those being sold have not been properly tested and are not authorised for use in the dental sector in the UK.

title: Public consultation on new pharmacovigilance legislation
description: How to comment on new pharmacovigilance legislation.

title: Implantable drug pumps for intrathecal therapy - risk of temporary or permanent neurological impairment
description: (All manufacturers) inflammatory or granulomatous mass formation at the catheter tip causes a risk of temporary or permanent neurological impairment. (MDA/2008/038)

title: Medicines related to valproate: risk of abnormal pregnancy outcomes
description: Children exposed in utero to valproate are at a high risk of serious developmental disorders (in up to 30-40% of cases) and/or congenital malformations (in approximately 10% of cases).

title: Valproate: re-analysis of study on risks in children of men taking valproate
description:
We are providing an update on a retrospective observational study on the risk to children born to men who took valproate in the 3 months before conception and on the need for the re-analysis of the data from this study before conclusions can be drawn. No action is needed from patients. It is vitally important that patients do not stop taking valproate unless they are advised by their specialist to do so. 

For female patients, healthcare professionals should continue to follow the existing strict precautions related to preventing the use of valproate in pregnancy (Valproate Pregnancy Prevention Programme).

title: Successful reclassification of Nasonex Allergy Control Nasal Spray
description: MHRA reclassifies Nasonex Allergy Control

title: Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies Annual Report 2024
description: Annual Report of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies - the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) and the British Pharmacopoeia Commission.

title: MHRA Safety Roundup: September 2025
description: Summary of the latest safety advice for medicines and medical device users

title: Adrenaline auto-injectors (AAIs): new guidance and resources for safe use
description: We inform healthcare professionals that the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has launched new guidance to highlight the latest safety advice on the steps to take during anaphylaxis. This new guidance includes an easy step-by-step guide on what to do in an emergency and provides updated advice on body positioning.

title: Coronavirus (COVID-19) and high blood pressure medication
description: There is no evidence from clinical or epidemiological studies that treatment with ACE-I or ARBs might worsen COVID-19 infection.

title: Letters sent to healthcare professionals in February 2016
description: In February 2016, a letter was sent to healthcare professionals regarding medicines containing valproate.

title: Bisphosphonates: use and safety
description: Guidance on the use and safety of bisphosphonates for bone-related conditions.

title: Consultation on Acnecide face gel and face wash
description: We are seeking views on a proposal to make Acnecide Face 5% w/w Gel and Acnecide Face Wash 5% w/w Gel available from general sales outlets.

title: Safety-critical alerts are changing at the MHRA
description: The MHRA will now issue all safety-critical alerts for medicines and medical devices that require action as National Patient Safety Alerts

title: Get more help to apply for medicines Integrated Scientific Advice (ISA)
description: Extra information for  the  Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) and National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE) Integrated Scientific Advice (ISA) service.

title: Early access to medicines scheme: expired scientific opinions
description: Medicines that were granted a positive opinion under the early access to medicines scheme (EAMS) which has now expired.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 14 February 2022
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 14 February 2022

title: Medical devices regulator issues alert on faulty glucose test strips
description: People with diabetes who use particular blood glucose test strips should stop using them and seek an alternative as soon as possible says the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA). This follows a manufacturing fault which could lead to a risk of them giving false low blood glucose results.

title: Statutory Instrument laid in Parliament sets out first steps in delivering Medical Device Regulatory Reform and strengthening patient safety
description: The new legislation will introduce clearer and risk-proportionate requirements that improve the safety of medical devices across Great Britain and provide certainty for manufacturers ahead of introducing the wider future regulatory regime.

title: Fast, Expert and Open – how the MHRA is poised to become a global leader in risk-proportionate regulation
description: New MHRA CEO puts safety, accelerated access and innovation at the centre of agency’s refreshed strategic direction.

title: Daclizumab (Zinbryta▼): suspension and recall for safety reasons; review patients as soon as possible and start alternative therapy
description: The European Medicines Agency (EMA) has recommended the immediate suspension of the marketing authorisation and recall of daclizumab (Zinbryta) in the EU following reports of serious inflammatory brain disorders, including encephalitis and meningoencephalitis, in patients with multiple sclerosis.

title: Innovation in regulation is shortening timelines for lowest risk clinical trials
description: Following MHRA approval of a clinical trial, a study on Nuwiq, a recombinant FVIII therapy for the treatment of haemophilia A, can proceed.

title: HRA and MHRA welcome public consultation on application of transparency rules of EU Clinical Trial Regulation
description: Health Research Authority (HRA) and Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) welcome public consultation.

title: Ponatinib (Iclusig▼): reports of posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome
description: Interrupt treatment if posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES) is confirmed and resume treatment only once the event is resolved and the benefit of continued treatment outweighs the risk of PRES.

title: Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies Annual Report 2021
description: Annual Report of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies - the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) and the British Pharmacopoeia Commission

title: New Years Honour for Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency Chief Executive
description: A statement from Dame June Raine DBE  on being recognised in the 2022 New Year Honours List

title: Dimethyl fumarate (Tecfidera): updated advice on risk of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy
description: Cases of progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy have been reported in patients taking dimethyl fumarate for multiple sclerosis, who all had prolonged lymphopenia.

title: Mycophenolate mofetil (CellCept) and mycophenolic acid: risk of hypogammaglobulinaemia and risk of bronchiectasis
description: Measure serum immunoglobulin levels if recurrent infections develop. Consider bronchiectasis or pulmonary fibrosis if patients develop persistent respiratory symptoms.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Sodium Benzoate (Amzoate) 2g in 10 mL Sterile Solution for injection, EL (20)A/24
description: Torbay Pharmaceuticals has informed us that the carton label on Sodium Benzoate (Amzoate) 2g in 10 mL Sterile Solution for injection incorrectly states the concentration of disodium edetate (excipient) as 0.1% w/v (10mg in 1mL) instead of 0.1% w/v (10mg in 10mL).

title: Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies Annual Report 2020
description: Annual Report of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies - the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) and the British Pharmacopoeia Commission

description: Annual report of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies - the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) and the British Pharmacopoeia Commission

title: Abacavir: risk of myocardial infarction—update from epidemiological studies
description: Abacavir should be used in line with current treatment guidelines and with caution in patients with high cardiovascular risk factors.

title: SGLT2 inhibitors: updated advice on increased risk of lower-limb amputation (mainly toes)
description: Canagliflozin may increase the risk of lower-limb amputation (mainly toes) in patients with type 2 diabetes. Evidence does not show an increased risk for dapagliflozin and empagliflozin, but the risk may be a class effect. Preventive foot care is important for all patients with diabetes.

description: Intrapharm Laboratories Ltd has informed us that there is a difference in dosage instructions between the carton and label. The Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) and bottle label contain the correct instructions.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Flamingo Pharma UK Ltd, Ibuprofen 400mg Tablets, EL (22)A/04
description: Flamingo Pharma UK Ltd have identified an error relating to the embossed batch number on some cartons of Ibuprofen 400mg tablets.

title: SGLT2 inhibitors: reports of Fournier’s gangrene (necrotising fasciitis of the genitalia or perineum)
description: If Fournier’s gangrene is suspected, stop the SGLT2 inhibitor and start treatment urgently (including antibiotics and surgical debridement). Fournier’s gangrene is a rare but potentially life-threatening infection that requires urgent medical attention.

title: Systemic and inhaled fluoroquinolones: small increased risk of aortic aneurysm and dissection; advice for prescribing in high-risk patients
description: In patients at risk for aortic aneurysm and dissection, fluoroquinolones should only be used after careful assessment of the benefits and risks and after consideration of other therapeutic options.

title: Valproate Pregnancy Prevention Programme: temporary advice for management during coronavirus (COVID-19)
description: Guidance has been published to support initiation of valproate in female patients and for annual review and pregnancy testing during the coronavirus pandemic.

description: Annual report of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies - the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) and the British Pharmacopoeia Commission

title: Recall – SAM XT Extremity Tourniquet
description: We are working with SAM Medical Products to recall affected tourniquets due to manufacture stitching error.

title: Amiodarone (Cordarone X): reminder of risks of treatment and need for patient monitoring and supervision
description: Amiodarone has been associated with serious and potentially life-threatening side effects, particularly of the lung, liver, and thyroid gland. We remind healthcare professionals that patients should be supervised and reviewed regularly during treatment. Lung problems may have slow onset but then progress rapidly. Computerised tomography scans may help to confirm a suspected diagnosis of pulmonary toxicity.

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 13 September 2021
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 13 September 2021

title: Drugs and driving: clarification for Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland
description: The new offence introduced in July does not apply in Northern Ireland nor replace any existing offences of driving.

title: Temporary waiver of 15-minute observation period after COVID-19 mRNA vaccines
description: A statement on the temporary waiver of 15-minute observation period after COVID-19 mRNA vaccines

title: Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies Annual Report 2023
description: Annual Report of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies - the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) and the British Pharmacopoeia Commission.

title: Proposal to end sell-through period for unlicensed herbal medicines
description: The MHRA is undertaking a public consultation on proposals to end the 'sell through' of unlicensed herbal remedies legally on market at April 2011.

title: Medicines licence submissions: compulsory use of electronic application forms (eAFs)
description: Use of electronic application forms (eAFs) is mandatory for all medicines licensing procedure types, including national procedures, from the beginning of 2016.

title: Four Non-Executive Directors appointed to the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency Board
description: Four Non-Executive Directors have been appointed to the board their terms come into effect from 1 September.

title: Proposal to make Dovonex Psoriasis Ointment available from Pharmacies
description: We are considering making Dovonex Psoriasis 50 microgram/g Ointment available to buy from pharmacies. We always want to involve the public and healthcare professionals in decisions that affect them. We want to know what you think about this change.

description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 29 August 2022.

title: Aliskiren (Rasilez▼): risk of angioedema and renal dysfunction
description: Use of aliskiren can cause angioedema, a rare and serious side effect, and or acute renal failure.

title: Voluntary recall of USA-manufactured homeopathic products: check for affected products
description: Certain batches of four homeopathic products manufactured in the USA have been voluntarily recalled due to possible microbial contamination.

title: MHRA’s new Chief Healthcare Quality and Access Officer begins important role
description: Dr Laura Squire OBE has begun her role as the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency’s (MHRA) Chief Healthcare Quality and Access Officer

title: Diclofenac: public consultation on availability as a pharmacy medicine
description: Public consultation launched on the continued availability of oral diclofenac as a pharmacy (P) medicine and on risk-minimisation measures advised by the Commission on Human Medicines.

description: Annual report of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies - the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) and the British Pharmacopoeia Commission

description: Annual report of the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 Advisory Bodies - the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) and the British Pharmacopoeia Commission

title: Skin creams dried on fabric can lead to fire deaths
description: A new campaign to raise awareness of the fire risk and the precautions that need to be taken by users of skin creams has been launched today

title: Dolutegravir (Tivicay▼, Triumeq▼, Juluca▼): updated advice on increased risk of neural tube defects
description: Updated safety recommendations have been issued as part of the European review evaluating cases of neural tube defects in babies born to mothers who became pregnant while taking the HIV medicine dolutegravir. Evidence collected as more women have given birth while on dolutegravir treatment shows a smaller increased risk than previously thought, almost comparable to other HIV drugs.  The previous restrictions against use in pregnancy are no longer in place – inform women of the potential risk of neural tube defects with dolutegravir and discuss the benefits and risks of continuing treatment if a woman plans pregnancy.

title: Exenatide (Byetta ▼): risk of severe pancreatitis and renal failure
description: There have been reports of necrotising and haemorrhagic pancreatitis in people taking exenatide some of which were fatal - stop exenatide treatment if pancreatitis is diagnosed.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Ennogen Pharma Limited, Trimogal 100mg and 200mg Tablets, EL (20)A/31
description: Ennogen Pharma Limited has informed us that the Patient Information Leaflets (PILs) within the packs for the affected batches below contain an error in Section 3 (How To Take Trimogal Tablets) with regard to the dosage instructions for children under 6 years.

title: Saquinavir: update on potential risk of arrhythmia
description: Saquinavir at a reduced dose for the first week of treatment

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Atnahs Pharma UK, Zestoretic 20mg/12.5mg Tablets, EL (23)A/05
description: Atnahs Pharma UK has informed the MHRA that the Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) packaged in certain batches of Zestoretic 20mg/12.5mg Tablets contains outdated safety information.

title: Regulator seizes almost 10,000 unsafe STI + HIV test kits
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) is warning people who buy STI test kits and HIV test kits online to make sure that they are purchasing from safe and legitimate sources

title: Direct-acting oral anticoagulants (DOACs): paediatric formulations; reminder of dose adjustments in patients with renal impairment
description: Risk minimisation materials are available to support the safe use of new paediatric formulations of rivaroxaban (Xarelto) and dabigatran etexilate (Pradaxa). In addition, we ask healthcare professionals to consult the current advice to ensure that all patients with renal impairment receive an appropriate dose of DOAC medicines.

title: MHRA announces a proposed framework for international recognition of medical devices
description: This initiative signals an important step towards a new regulatory framework for medical devices in Great Britain which protects public health, ensures access to MedTech innovations, and maintains an attractive market for industry

title: Diabetics warned of health risks of suddenly changing life-saving devices without medical supervision
description: People with diabetes are being reminded to seek medical advice before changing their insulin therapy devices.

description: Pfizer Limited has informed us that the packs for the affected batches below have not been packaged with the current version of the Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) containing updated storage instructions of the infusion solution in section 5.

title: Medical devices regulations: targeted consultation on the indefinite recognition of CE marked devices
description: This consultation invites views on proposals for recognising CE marked medical devices in Great Britain.

title: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency Privacy Notice
description: This privacy notice describes how we collect and use your personal information, in accordance with the Data Protection Act and the General Data Protection Regulation.

description: Calcitonin-containing medicines should no longer be used in treatment of osteoporosis.

title: Clopidogrel and proton pump inhibitors: interaction—updated advice
description: In light of the most recent evidence, the previous advice on the concomitant use of clopidogrel with proton pump inhibitors has now been modified. Use of either omeprazole or esomeprazole with clopidogrel should be discouraged. The current evidence does not support extending this advice to other PPIs

title: Recall of GlucaGen® HypoKits used by diabetics in an emergency
description: People with diabetes are being asked to check whether they have certain batches of GlucaGen® HypoKits used for the emergency treatment of severe low blood glucose (hypoglycaemia).

title: Aripiprazole (Abilify and generic brands): risk of pathological gambling
description: Healthcare professionals prescribing aripiprazole are reminded to be alert to the risk of addictive gambling and other impulse control disorders. Healthcare professionals should advise patients, their families and friends to be alert to these risks.

title: New Chair of the MHRA
description: Anthony Harnden has been appointed the new Chair of the Medicine and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA).

title: Proton pump inhibitors: very low risk of subacute cutaneous lupus erythematosus
description: Proton pump inhibitors (PPIs) are associated with very infrequent cases of subacute cutaneous lupus erythematosus (SCLE), a non-scarring dermatosis that can develop in sun-exposed areas.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Orifarm UK Ltd, Concerta XL 18mg & 36 mg prolonged release tablets, EL (24)A/07
description: Orifarm UK has informed the MHRA of an error with the Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) packaged within the parallel import packs of the specific batches of Concerta XL 18mg and 36mg prolonged release tablets mentioned in this notification.

title: Trametinib (Mekinist▼): risk of gastrointestinal perforation and colitis
description: Use trametinib, authorised either as monotherapy or combined with dabrafenib, with caution in patients with risk factors for gastrointestinal perforation.

title: MHRA reaffirms safety of childhood vaccination
description: Vaccination is one of the most effective and safest ways to protect against many serious infectious diseases

title: MHRA designated as WHO-Listed Authority: a milestone for UK life sciences and global health
description: WHO recognition affirms MHRA’s commitment to regulatory excellence, innovation and global collaboration

title: MHRA’s 2024–25 Annual Report and Accounts and Impact Report show progress on safety, innovation, and regulatory excellence
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has published its 2024–25 Annual Report and Accounts, and accompanying Impact Report.

title: New research shows desperate dieters in danger -  the secret world of online slimming pills
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) is working in partnership with Slimming World as part of the Agency’s wider #FakeMeds Campaign to encourage those wanting to lose weight to turn to safe, legitimate and appropriate routes.

title: MHRA pilots patient involvement in new applications
description: A pilot project that puts patient involvement at the heart of clinical trials and medicine development has been launched by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA).

title: Topical ketoprofen: reminder on risk of photo-sensitivity reactions
description: A reminder for those who use topical ketoprofen to avoid direct sunlight, ultraviolet rays, sunbeds or sunlamps.

description: The meeting, which took place at the Royal College of Surgeons in Edinburgh, centred on the MHRA’s commitment to delivering the agency’s priorities in Scotland

description: UK companies who have their licence to manufacture or wholesale medicines terminated and cancelled.

description: A new, visionary Corporate Plan, setting out how the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency plans to keep patients safe by enabling access to innovative, safe and effective medical products over the coming three years, has been published today (4 July 2023).

description: An application for exceptional use of a COVID-19 rapid test, to be used by members of the public, has today been approved by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA).

title: Canagliflozin (Invokana▼, Vokanamet▼): signal of increased risk of lower extremity amputations observed in trial in high cardiovascular risk patients
description: A signal of increased lower limb amputation (primarily of the toe) in people taking canagliflozin compared with placebo in a clinical trial in high cardiovascular risk patients is currently under investigation.

description: Details and statistics on the import of unlicensed medicines from March 2014 onwards.

description: Temporary safety measures are in place while an EU review investigates the link between cases of serious liver injury, including 4 cases requiring liver transplantation, and Esmya for uterine fibroids.

title: Integrated Yellow Card reporting now available in 93% of GP practices in the UK

description: Osteonecrosis of the external auditory canal has been reported very rarely (fewer than 1 in 10 000 patients) with bisphosphonates, mainly in association with long-term therapy (2 years or longer).

description: Information for healthcare professionals and the public about the COVID-19 vaccine Nuvaxovid.

description: Report of the Isotretinoin Implementation Advisory Expert Working Group regarding recommendations to strengthen the safe use of isotretinoin, the acne medicine commonly known by brand names Roaccutane and Reticutan.

description: Strandhaven Limited t/a Somex Pharma has informed the MHRA regarding an error with the Patient Information Leaflets (PILs) that have been packed in the listed batches of Tramadol Hydrochloride 50mg Capsules, Hard.

title: Fingolimod (Gilenya▼): increased risk of congenital malformations; new contraindication during pregnancy and in women of childbearing potential not using effective contraception
description: Fingolimod is associated with an increased risk of major congenital malformations including cardiac, renal, and musculoskeletal defects, when used in pregnancy. Women of childbearing potential must use effective contraception during fingolimod treatment and for 2 months after discontinuation.

title: Hepatitis B vaccines for babies
description: All babies in the UK get the hepatitis B vaccine. It has a well-established safety record and ensures long-term protection against the virus.
