description: Topical oral salicylate gels are no longer indicated for people younger than 16 years for pain associated with infant teething, orthodontic devices, cold sores, or mouth ulcers.

title: Tramadol 50mg capsules - risk of fungal contamination
description: (RelonChem Ltd/ Kent Pharmaceuticals) All unexpired batches of 50mg Tramadol capsules are being recalled due to the risk of fungal contamination. Class 2 – action within 48 hours. (EL (15)A/04)

title: Human papillomavirus immunisation programme
description: Second year safety review

title: Insulins (all types): risk of cutaneous amyloidosis at injection site
description: Cutaneous amyloidosis at the injection site has been reported in patients using insulin and this may affect glycaemic control. Remind patients to rotate injection sites within the same body region.

title: Isotretinoin – updates to prescribing guidance and survey of services
description: The Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) has endorsed changes to isotretinoin prescribing guidance. In addition, CHM is seeking further information from dermatology services who prescribe isotretinoin to inform any future changes to current risk minimisation measures.

title: Questran 4g sachet Powder for Oral Suspension - possible contamination with Enterococcus faecium
description: (Bristol-Myers Squibb Pharmaceuticals Limited) Source of possible contamination is believed to be sucrose used within this formulation of Questran – class 2 action within 48 hours.  (EL (13)A/15)

title: Intra-oral dental X-ray units: Kodak 2100 and Kodak 2200 - risk of injury
description: (Carestream Health) Risk of injury if joint between scissor arm and bracket fails. (MDA/2014/005)

title: Daclizumab (Zinbryta▼) and risk of severe liver injury: new restrictions to use and strengthened liver monitoring
description: The use of daclizumab (daclizumab beta) is now restricted to adults with relapsing multiple sclerosis who have had an inadequate response to at least 2 other disease-modifying therapies (DMTs) and for whom other DMTs are contraindicated or unsuitable. Do not use daclizumab in patients with pre-existing hepatic disease or hepatic impairment. Discuss with patients the risk of hepatic injury and the liver monitoring requirements before starting or continuing daclizumab and ask them to sign an acknowledgement form to confirm they understand the information.

title: All types, makes and models of breast implants - potential increased risk of anaplastic large cell lymphoma (ALCL)
description: (All) uncertain evidence that women with breast implants may have a very small but increased risk of anaplastic large cell lymphoma (ALCL) of the breast - none reported in the UK. (MDA/2011/017)

title: Lipid Phase only of Parenteral Nutrition - potential contamination with Bacillus cereus
description: (ITH Pharma Ltd) Small number of reported cases of bacillus cereus septicaemia affecting neonates - class 1 action now. (EL (14)A/05)

title: Daclizumab beta (Zinbryta▼): risk of immune-mediated encephalitis – some cases several months after stopping treatment
description: Monitoring for encephalitis should continue for 12 months following discontinuation of daclizumab. Inform all patients who have discontinued daclizumab and their caregivers of the common symptoms of encephalitis and the need to contact their doctor immediately if they occur.

title: MHRA outlines intent to speed up patient access to innovative medical devices
description: Statement of Policy Intent sets out initial thinking on a new Early Access service to help patients benefit sooner from innovative medical devices that address unmet clinical needs.

title: Certificate of Free Sale to be issued by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) will start issuing Certificates of Free Sale (CFS) from Wednesday 1 April 2015.

title: Professor Sir Aziz Sheikh: Towards next-generation pharmacovigilance capabilities for the UK
description: As medicines and medical technologies become more complex, there are exciting opportunities to further strengthen our safety monitoring systems.

title: Brimonidine gel (Mirvaso): risk of systemic cardiovascular effects; not to be applied to damaged skin
description: Systemic cardiovascular effects including bradycardia, hypotension, and dizziness have been reported after application. It is important to avoid application to irritated or damaged skin, including after laser therapy.

title: Dopamine agonists: pathological gambling, increased libido, and hypersexuality
description: Healthcare professionals  should warn patients that compulsive behaviour with dopamine agonists may be dose-related.

title: Levonorgestrel and ulipristal remain suitable emergency contraceptives for all women
description: Emergency contraceptives remain suitable for all women regardless of the woman’s weight or body mass index (BMI).

title: Aventis Pharma Limited trading as Sanofi Rifadin 600mg Infusion
description: Sanofi is recalling the A5545 batch of Rifadin because an investigation of out of trend results indicated that it was contaminated with chilled water circulating in the jacket of the reactor during manufacture. (EL(16)A/04)

title: New MedRegs blog
description: MHRA has launched an official blog providing expert insight into the latest regulatory thinking and all aspects of medicines regulation.

title: Class 2 medicines recall: GlucaGen HypoKit 1mg (glucagon) PL 04668/0027 (GlucaGen 1mg) PL 04668/0028 (Solvent for GlucaGen 1mg)
description: Action within 48 hours: Novo Nordisk Ltd. is recalling seven batches of GlucaGen HypoKit due to a small number of needles being detached from the syringe.

title: Buccolam Oromucosal Solution - possibility for chemical contamination
description: (ViroPharma SPRL) Pharmacists should quarantine batches and return them to original wholesaler for credit - class 2 action within 48 hours. (EL (14)A/12)

title: Suzhou Surgicare disposable Hysteroscopy Sheath– Recall due to withdrawn CE certificate, (DSI/2025/001)
description: The MHRA has become aware of Hysteroscopy Sheaths supplied in the UK market with a withdrawn CE certificate. Healthcare professionals and providers should immediately stop use, quarantine, and stop supply of any identified product(s).

title: MHRA highlights “remarkable” progress and launches real-world data consultation on International Clinical Trials Day
description: “…the MHRA is once again taking a global lead” says Lord O’Shaughnessy

title: Devices used for endometrial ablation - clinicians should confirm patients have no evidence of uterine perforation or false passage
description: (All makes and models) MHRA receives reports of uterine wall injury, wall perforation or the creation of a false passage after use of endometrial ablation devices. (MDA/2010/006).

title: St John’s wort: interaction with hormonal contraceptives, including implants
description: St John’s wort interacts with hormonal contraceptives reducing the effectiveness and increasing the risk of unplanned pregnancy.

title: Valproate medicines and serious harms in pregnancy: new Annual Risk Acknowledgement Form and clinical guidance from professional bodies to support compliance with the Pregnancy Prevention Programme
description:
Ongoing patient survey data suggest that more effort is needed by clinicians to achieve full and timely compliance with the valproate Pregnancy Prevention Programme and meet the goal to rapidly reduce and eventually eliminate the harms of valproate in pregnancy in view of its serious teratogenicity. 
We have updated the Annual Risk Acknowledgement Form, which should be used during annual specialist review of all women and girls of childbearing potential on valproate medicines (irrespective of indication). Specialists should comply with guidance given on the form if they consider the patient is not at risk of pregnancy, including the need for regular review in case her risk status changes.

title: Panitumumab (Vectibix): serious hypersensitivity reactions
description: New reports of serious hypersensitivity reactions (including anaphylaxis) in patients receiving panitumumab, some of which were fatal.

title: Crestor 5mg Tablets Parallel Imported by BR Lewis Pharmaceuticals Ltd (Originator name Provisacor) –rogue blister and carton
description: Potentially a rogue blister and carton may be present in a batch of Crestor 5mg Tablets Parallel Imported by BR Lewis Pharmaceuticals Ltd (Originator name Provisacor) – Class 4 Caution in Use. (EL (16)A/03)

title: Freedom of Information responses from the MHRA - week commencing 19 December 2022
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) responses to freedom of information (FOI) requests for week commencing 19 December 2022.

title: Class 2 Medicines Recall: Diclo-SR 75 Tablets (Diclofenac Sodium)
description: Strides Pharma UK Ltd, trading as Co-pharma, is recalling specific batches of Diclo-SR 75 Tablets as a precautionary measure

title: Epistatus 10mg in 1ml Oromucosal Solution - fault with neck adaptor
description: (Special Products Ltd) A batch of Epistatus 10mg in 1ml Oromucosal Solution is being recalled due to the wrong size of neck adaptor in some bottles (EL (16)A/06)

title: Vimpat 150mg Tablets - blister strips are incorrectly labelled
description: (OPD Laboratories Limited) Recall as some packs have incorrectly-labelled blister strips – class 2 action within 48 hours. (EL (13)A/01)

title: Levonorgestrel-releasing intrauterine systems: prescribe by brand name
description: Levonorgestrel-releasing intrauterine systems should always be prescribed by brand name because products have different indications, durations of use, and introducers.

title: Class 2 Medicines Recall: Lipitor (Atorvastatin) 80mg film-coated tablets / Atorvastatin 80mg film-coated tablets, Almus livery
description: Pfizer is recalling the above batches as a precaution due to an out of specification result for microbiological testing during routine stability studies on a batch of the product which was not released to the market. (EL (17) A/12)

title: Apremilast (Otezla ▼): risk of suicidal thoughts and behaviour
description: There is an increased risk that some patients may experience psychiatric symptoms with apremilast, including depression and suicidal thoughts. Stop treatment if patients have new psychiatric symptoms or if existing symptoms worsen.

title: Levonorgestrel-releasing intrauterine systems: prescribe by brand name
description: Levonorgestrel-releasing intrauterine systems should always be prescribed by brand name because products have different indications, durations of use, and introducers.

title: Class 2 Medicines Recall: Lipitor (Atorvastatin) 80mg film-coated tablets / Atorvastatin 80mg film-coated tablets, Almus livery
description: Pfizer is recalling the above batches as a precaution due to an out of specification result for microbiological testing during routine stability studies on a batch of the product which was not released to the market. (EL (17) A/12)

title: Class 2 medicines recall: Respontin Nebules 250micrograms/1ml & Respontin Nebules 500micrograms/2ml (Ipratropium Bromide)
description: Action within 48 hours: Glaxo Wellcome UK Ltd is recalling 4 batches of Respontin Nebules due to out of specification results for impurities obtained during routine stability testing (EL (16)A/10)

title: Class 2 Medicines Recall: Fiasp FlexTouch 100 units/mL solution for injection pre-filled pen manufactured by Novo Nordisk (MDR 065-06/18)
description: Novo Nordisk is recalling one batch as a precautionary measure due to the presence of particles in a small number of samples.

title: MHRA FOI performance data
description: Performance data relating to the handling of Freedom of Information requests to the Medicines and Healthcare Regulatory Agency (MHRA).

title: Five non-executive directors reappointed to the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency Board
description: Two board members have been reappointed for two years, while three others have had their term extended by a year.

title: Prasugrel (Efient▼): rare but serious hypersensitivity reactions
description: Healthcare professionals should be aware of this risk when prescribing prasugrel.

title: Capecitabine: risk of severe skin reactions - discontinue treatment
description: Severe skin reactions such as Stevens-Johnson syndrome and toxic epidermal necrolysis have been reported during treatment with capecitabine.

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Advanz Pharma  Zapain 30mg/500mg Tablets (Codeine Phosphate /Paracetamol), PL12762/0034, (EL(20)A/01)
description: Caution in Use: Distribute to Pharmacy level. Advanz Pharma has informed us of a discrepancy on the product packaging. The discrepancy relates to “capsules” printed at the top right of the packs in error, instead of tablets

title: Febuxostat: updated advice for the treatment of patients with a history of major cardiovascular disease
description: Caution is required if prescribing febuxostat in patients with pre-existing major cardiovascular disease, particularly, in those with evidence of high urate crystal and tophi burden or those initiating urate-lowering therapy.

title: Ustekinumab (Stelara): risk of exfoliative dermatitis
description: If you suspect exfoliative dermatitis caused by an adverse drug reaction to ustekinumab, stop treatment.

title: Latanoprost (Xalatan): increased reporting of eye irritation since reformulation
description: Advise patients to tell their health professional if they experience severe eye irritation.

title: Roflumilast (Daxas▼): risk of suicidal behaviour
description: Avoid use in patients with previous or existing psychiatric symptoms and discontinue treatment if new or worsening psychiatric symptoms occur.

title: Reusable transoesophageal echocardiography, transvaginal and transrectal ultrasound probes (transducers) – failure to appropriately decontaminate
description: (Invacare) Should be appropriately decontaminated between each patient use. (MDA/2012/038)

title: Sprint Fidelis implantable cardioverter defibrillator (ICD) lead - risk of inappropriate shocks
description: (Medtronic) use of Sprint Fidelis ICD lead could create risk of inappropriate shocks and loss of therapy with model numbers 6930, 6931, 6948 and 6949. (MDA/2011/051)

title: Class 4 Medicines Defect Information: Depo-Provera 150mg/ml Injection (1ml Vial) EL (20)A/26
description: Drugsrus Ltd (distributor) / Tenolol Ltd (MA Holder) has informed us of a typographical error in active ingredient stated on the label on the vial.

title: Bydureon 2mg powder and solvent - may contain small number of under-filled vials
description: (Eli Lilly Nederland B.V.) Remaining stocks of affected batch should be quarantined and returned to original supplier for credit – class 2 action within 48 hours. (EL (13)A/22)

title: MHRA delivers guidance on human factors
description: In collaboration with key stakeholders, MHRA has produced guidance on the human factors aspects of design for medical devices including those in drug-device combination products.

title: Roche CoaguChek device producing inaccurate results
description: MHRA are asking people who use the CoaguChek device to check their test strips as a precaution following an alert by the manufacturer Roche.

title: Global AI challenge to investigate veterans’ cardiovascular health launched
description: A brand-new challenge for artificial intelligence and machine learning developers, to build models that can predict heart failure-related health outcomes for veterans has been launched by a collaboration between regulators and healthcare organisations in the UK and US.

title: Consultation with healthcare professionals: please complete our consultation to help influence how we communicate with you
description: The MHRA is reviewing its approach to engaging with healthcare professionals on the safety of medicines and medical devices. Through our consultation, you can provide your views and help inform our new approach.

title: Class 4 defect information: Amoxicillin 500mg Capsules BP (MDR 92-12/18)
description:
Accord Healthcare Limited has informed us that that a change to the Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) for
this product has not been implemented by the required timeline.

title: Recombinant human erythropoietins: treating anaemia in cancer
description: Patients with cancer who received recombinant human erythropoietins in clinical trials had an increased risk of tumour progression and reduced overall survival compared with study controls.

title: Desmomelt (desmopressin): report incidents on yellow card portal
description: Healthcare professionals are encouraged to report all suspected adverse reactions (ADRs) on the yellow card porftal to melt formulations of desmopressin.

title: Polio detection times could be halved using a scientific detection method, new research shows
description: New research supported by the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has shown that the time to detect polio can be halved using a technique called Direct molecular Detection and Nanopore Sequencing (DDNS), supporting the global effort to eradicate polio and help save lives.

title: Reducing Regulatory Burden
description: Report outlining the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency’s (MHRA's) commitment to burden reduction activity.

title: Global impact of UK health data resource highlighted in newly published paper
description: New analysis shows Clinical Practice Research Datalink has supported groundbreaking medical research across 29 countries with close to 3,800 published studies

title: Batches of Durex condoms recalled: people urged to check their batch numbers
description: Anyone using affected batches are advised to stop use over concerns they do not meet safety standards.

title: Stephen Lightfoot to become Chair
description: Stephen Lightfoot will become Chair of the Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) from 1 September 2020.

title: Implantable cardioverter defibrillators (ICDs) - disable all high voltage shock therapies before you remove ICD
description: (All manufacturers and models) There is a risk of electric shock for clinicians or mortuary personnel while they remove implantable cardioverter defibrillators (ICDs). (MDA/2008/068)

title: Agomelatine (Valdoxan): risk of liver toxicity
description: Reminder to test liver function before and during treatment.

title: Class 4 medicines defect information: Inhixa solution for injection in pre-filled syringe (EL (18)A/07)
description: Caution in Use: Distribute to Pharmacy and Clinic Level.

title: Brochlor 1% w/w Eye Ointment – issue with seal integrity and compromised sterility assurance
description: (Sanofi-aventis) Remaining stocks of the affected batches should be quarantined and returned to the original supplier for credit – class 2 action within 48 hours. (EL (13)A/07)

title: Canesten Pessary - 2 batches have wrong patient information leaflet
description: (Bayer Consumer Care Division) Two batches inadvertently placed on the market containing patient information leaflet (PIL) for prescription only medicine (POM) product - class 4 caution in use. (EL(11)A/17)

title: Orciprenaline sulphate (Alupent): withdrawal due to unfavourable benefit-risk profile
description: Patients who require a liquid oral formulation of a β-agonist should be switched to a more-selective short-acting β2-agonist such as salbutamol or terbutaline.

title: Defibrillators/monitors used for external pacing with non-invasive transcutaneous pacemaker modules - risk of inadequate external pacing
description: (All manufacturers and models) Can lead to potential periods of asystole, syncope and possible death. (MDA/2013/032)

title: Retrievable inferior vena cava (IVC) filters - serious complications associated with attempted IVC filter retrieval
description: (All models and manufacturers) Be aware of the various maximum implant durations for safe retrieval for each IVC filter model. (MDA/2013/028)

title: Nitrous oxide: neurological and haematological toxic effects
description: Toxic effects can occur with nitrous oxide especially with prolonged use.

title: MHRA information on TPP and QRISK®2
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) investigated an issue involving a digital calculator used by some GPs to assess the potential risk of heart disease in patients.

title: Pomalidomide (Imnovid▼): risk of hepatitis B reactivation
description: Before starting treatment with pomalidomide, establish hepatitis B virus status in all patients.

title: Montelukast 5 mg Chewable Tablets - error on the carton
description: (Mylan) Carton incorrectly states "For children 2 to 5 years" but patient information leaflet (PIL) correctly states "For children 6 to 14 years" – class 2 action within 48 hours. (EL (13)A/08)

title: Clopixol Conc Injection (500mg/ml) - cracked ampoules
description: (Lundbeck Limited) Reports of cracked ampoules and concerns about guaranteeing sterility – class 2 actions within 48 hours (EL (11)A/33)

title: Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors: serious cases of keratitis and ulcerative keratitis
description: Patients who present with acute or worsening signs and symptoms of keratitis should be referred promptly to an ophthalmology specialist.

title: Ikervis 1 mg/ml eye drops, emulsion (ciclosporin) - micro particles of active ingredient
description: (Santen UK Ltd) Two batches of Ikervis 1 mg/ml eye drops, emulsion (30 single dose containers) (ciclosporin), are being recalled because micro particles of the active ingredient were found in stability test samples (EL(16)A/07)

title: MHRA statement on COVID-19 booster vaccines
description: Regulatory updates by the MHRA today on the COVID-19 booster vaccine programme for winter 2021 to 2022

title: Abrysvo▼ (Pfizer RSV vaccine) and Arexvy▼ (GSK RSV vaccine): be alert to a small risk of Guillain-Barré syndrome following vaccination in older adults
description: There is a small increase in the risk of Guillain-Barré syndrome following vaccination with Abrysvo (Pfizer respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccine) and Arexvy (GSK RSV vaccine) in adults aged 60 years and older.  Healthcare professionals should advise all recipients of Abrysvo and Arexvy that they should be alert to signs and symptoms of Guillain-Barré syndrome and, if they occur, to seek immediate medical attention as it requires urgent treatment in hospital.

title: Duloxetine: marketed as Cymbalta▼ and Yentreve▼ for different disorders
description: Patients should be assessed regularly due to reported cases of suicidal ideation and suicidal behaviour during treatment with duloxetine or shortly after stopping treatment.

title: Prasugrel (Efient): increased risk of bleeding
description: New clinical trial information is available on the timing of the loading dose of prasugrel when used in patients with unstable angina or non-ST segment elevation myocardial infarction.

title: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency board meetings: pilot of public sessions
description: The agency will pilot holding board meetings in public in 2016.

title: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency: board meetings in public session in 2016
description: The Agency is holding a second public session of its board meeting. Register now to attend the meeting in April.

title: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency annual report and accounts 2005 to 2006
description: This document contains the following information: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency annual report and accounts 2005 to 2006

title: Botulinum toxin type B (Neurobloc): serious known risks
description: Risks such as toxin spread reported mostly with off-label use.

title: Field Safety Notices - 26 - 30 December 2016
description: Summary List of field safety notices (FSNs) from medical device manufacturers from 26-30 December 2016

title: Cytarabine Injection 100mg/ml (1g/10ml) - potential for crystallisation
description: (Hospira UK Limited) Visually inspect vials for particulate matter prior to use and do not use if crystals are found - Class 4 medicines defect information (EL (13)A/09)

title: Class 3 Medicines Recall: Accord Healthcare Limited, Amlodipine 10mg Tablets
description: Accord Healthcare Limited is recalling a specific batch of Amlodipine 10mg Tablets as a precautionary measure due to out of specification results obtained during stability testing.

title: Ergot-derived dopamine agonists: risk of fibrotic reactions
description: New warnings and contraindications for ergot-derived dopamine agonists due to risk of fibrosis associated with chronic use.

title: Valproate (Belvo, Convulex, Depakote, Dyzantil, Epilim, Epilim Chrono or  Chronosphere, Episenta, Epival, and Syonell▼): review by  two specialists is required for initiating valproate but not for  male patients already taking valproate
description: Review by two specialists remains in place for patients initiating valproate under 55 years of age but the Commission on Human Medicines (CHM) has advised that it will not be required for men (or males) currently taking valproate. Three infographics have been developed to provide clarity regarding valproate prescribing.

title: Implantable cardioverter defibrillators (ICDs) - ensure you have updated software for models 3510 and 3510+ ICD programmers
description: (St Jude Medical) potential reduction in number of shocks (skipped charge) delivered per therapy episode and inappropriate rate responsive pacing for up to 90 minutes. (MDA/2005/045)

title: Tofacitinib (Xeljanz▼): new measures to minimise risk of venous thromboembolism and of serious and fatal infections
description: Caution should be used in patients with known risk factors for venous thromboembolism in addition to the underlying disease. Patients older than 65 years of age are at an increased risk of serious infections and should be treated with tofacitinib only if there is no alternative treatment.

title: Systemically administered VEGF pathway inhibitors: risk of aneurysm and artery dissection
description: Before initiating systemic vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) pathway inhibitors, carefully consider the risk of aneurysm and artery dissection in patients with risk factors. In patients who receive a systemic VEGF pathway inhibitor, reduce as far as possible any modifiable risk factors such as hypertension.

title: Dipeptidylpeptidase-4 inhibitors: risk of acute pancreatitis
description: There have been reports of acute pancreatitis associated with drugs in the dipeptidylpeptidase-4 (DPP-4) inhibitor class of antidiabetic agents (‘gliptins’).

title: Isoline implantable cardioverter defibrillator (ICD) leads - risk of inappropriate shocking, pacing inhibition or shocking inhibition
description: (Hospira) Due to insulation abrasion. (MDA/2013/007)

title: Lemtrada (alemtuzumab) and serious cardiovascular and immune-mediated adverse reactions: new restrictions to use and strengthened monitoring requirements
description: While an urgent EU safety review evaluates reports of serious cardiovascular events and immune-mediated reactions, including autoimmune hepatitis, the use of alemtuzumab (Lemtrada) has been restricted and strengthened requirements have been introduced to monitor vital signs and liver function before and during treatment. All patients on alemtuzumab for multiple sclerosis should be alerted to these risks and what to do if symptoms occur.

title: Macrolane™ Volume Restoration Factor (VRF) dermal filler for breast augmentation - diagnosis of breast cancer difficult in some cases
description: (Q-Med AB - a Galderma division) problem with use of Macrolane VRF20 and Macrolane VRF30 for breast augmentation which can make diagnosis of breast cancer more difficult. (MDA/2012/022)

title: Register medical devices to place on the market
description: How to register your medical devices with the MHRA for the markets in Great Britain and Northern Ireland.

title: Unicondylar sled knee prosthesis - risk of early wear
description: (Waldemar Link GmbH & Company.KG) Consider annual review of patients for up to 5 years after implantation, including X-rays to detect progression of bearing wear. (MDA/2014/018)

title: The importance of giving patients a voice in the approval of new sickle cell treatment
description: This case study is an example of how the MHRA is benefiting from patients providing their lived experience for the pre-authorisation stage of the benefit-risk review.

title: Aurum pre-filled syringes phased introduction of new 10ml CONNECT syringe barrel: Importance of selecting compatible needle-free connectors to minimise the risk of syringe blockage (DSI/2025/02)
description: Due to a phased introduction of the new 10ml CONNECT barrel design for the Aurum pre-filled syringes, the range of compatible needle free connectors (NFCs) differs between the established syringe barrel and the new syringe barrel. It is vital that healthcare professionals use compatible NFCs with Aurum pre-filled syringes to prevent delays to delivery of emergency medication.

title: Antipsychotics: risk of venous thromboembolic events
description: Use of Antipsychotic may be associated with an increased risk of venous thromboembolic events.

title: Class 2 medicines recall: Nacrez 75 microgram Film-coated Tablets (Desogestrel) PL 00289/1706
description: Action Within 48 Hours. Wholesaler and Pharmacy Level Recall. Teva UK Limited is recalling batches due to out of specification results for impurities obtained during routine stability testing.

title: Human papillomavirus vaccine Cervarix: balance of risks and benefits remains clearly positive
description: A safety review conducted at the end of its routine use during the ongoing human papillomavirus immunisation programme has found that no new risks have been identified for Cervarix, and that the balance of its risks and benefits remains clearly positive. Cervarix was replaced in the programme by the HPV vaccine Gardasil▼ from September 2012.

title: Improving how we collect and document consent
description: The Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) and the Health Research Authority (HRA) have published a joint statement on seeking and documenting consent using electronic methods (eConsent).

title: Clozapine: reminder of potentially fatal risk of intestinal obstruction, faecal impaction, and paralytic ileus
description: If constipation occurs during treatment with clozapine (Clozaril, Denzapine, Zaponex), it is vital that it is recognised and actively treated.

title: Good Machine Learning Practice for Medical Device Development: Guiding Principles
description: Ten guiding principles that can inform the development of Good Machine Learning Practice (GMLP).

title: MHRA warns of unsafe counterfeit anti-choking devices
description: Thousands of counterfeit or unbranded copies of LifeVac anti-choking devices are being sold across the UK. The public is reminded to check their product carefully against the images in MHRA guidance to identify whether it is a counterfeit.

title: Denosumab (Prolia, Xgeva▼): reports of osteonecrosis of the external auditory canal
description: Denosumab is associated with a risk of osteonecrosis of the jaw, osteonecrosis of the external auditory canal has also been reported with denosumab.

title: Oseltamivir (Tamiflu): changed concentration and dosing dispenser of oral suspension
description: Healthcare professionals should advise patients and/or carers of the changes in the dose, packaging, dose dispenser and leaflet.

title: Recall of Human Albumin Biotest CLDA (17)A/02
description: Biotest UK Ltd is recalling the listed batches due to low level contamination which occurred during manufacture

title: Codeine: restricted use as analgesic in children and adolescents after European safety review
Warfarin: reports of calciphylaxis
description: Calciphylaxis is a very rare but serious condition causing vascular calcification and skin necrosis.

title: New synthetic datasets to assist COVID-19 and cardiovascular research
description: Ground-breaking innovation to support medical technologies

title: Rivaroxaban (Xarelto▼): reminder that 15 mg and 20 mg tablets should be taken with food
description: MHRA has received a small number of reports suggesting lack of efficacy (thromboembolic events) in patients taking 15 mg or 20 mg rivaroxaban on an empty stomach; remind patients to take 15 mg or 20 mg rivaroxaban tablets with food.

title: Riociguat (Adempas): not for use in patients with pulmonary hypertension associated with idiopathic interstitial pneumonias
description: Patients with pulmonary hypertension associated with idiopathic interstitial pneumonias should not be treated with riociguat in light of interim results from a recently terminated study.

title: Hydrocortisone muco-adhesive buccal tablets: should not be used off-label for adrenal insufficiency in children due to serious risks
description: Risk of insufficient cortisol absorption and life-threatening adrenal crisis if muco-adhesive buccal tablets are used as adrenal replacement therapy.

title: Modified COVID-19 vaccines for variants to be fast-tracked, says MHRA and other regulators
description: Future vaccine modifications that respond to new variants of coronavirus to be made available quickly to UK recipients, without compromising on safety, quality or effectiveness.

title: Risk-Adapted Approach to clinical trials and Risk Assessments
description: The MHRA have implemented a dual strategy for a risk-adapted approach to clinical trials in the UK.

title: Implantable pacemakers - residual longevity overestimated
description: (Sorin) Difference between predicted and actual pacemaker battery depletion characteristics. (MDA/2013/084)

title: Parenteral amphotericin B: reminder of risk of potentially fatal adverse reaction if formulations confused
description: Following receipt of a third case of fatal medication error caused by the administration of Fungizone (a non-lipid-based formulation of amphotericin B) instead of a lipid-based formulation (AmBisome, Abelcet), we remind healthcare professionals that these formulations are not interchangeable. Prescribers, pharmacists, and nurses need to be fully aware of the formulation being used and the associated dose regimen.

title: Good manufacturing practice inspection deficiencies
description: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) good manufacturing practice (GMP) deficiencies since April 2010.

title: Xofigo▼ (radium-223-dichloride): new restrictions on use due to increased risk of fracture and trend for increased mortality seen in clinical trial
description: Now only authorised for use in patients with symptomatic bone metastases and no known visceral metastases who have had 2 previous systemic treatments for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer or who cannot receive other systemic treatments. Do not use in combination with abiraterone acetate and prednisone/prednisolone.

title: Statins: updated adverse reaction information in patient leaflets
description: Healthcare professionals should be aware of the updated information so they can discuss it with new and existing patients.

title: Epoetin beta (NeoRecormon): increased risk of retinopathy in preterm infants cannot be excluded
description: Possible increased risk of retinopathy with epoetin beta in premature infants calls for careful consideration of options for preventing anaemia of prematurity.

title: Breast implants - report cases of anaplastic large cell lymphoma (ALCL)

title: Filgrastim and pegfilgrastim: risk of capillary leak syndrome
description:
Capillary leak syndrome (CLS) has been reported in recipients of filgrastim, including patients undergoing chemotherapy and a healthy donor undergoing peripheral blood progenitor-cell mobilisation; it has also been reported in recipients of pegfilgrastim undergoing chemotherapy. Episodes varied in severity and frequency. CLS is characterised by: hypotension and oedema; hypoalbuminaemia; and haemoconcentration, and may be fatal unless promptly diagnosed and managed.

Prescribers should monitor patients and healthy donors for signs and symptoms of capillary leak syndrome (CLS), and should give standard symptomatic treatment immediately if symptoms occur.

title: Gentamicin: potential for histamine-related adverse drug reactions with some batches

title: Intravenous zoledronic acid: adverse effects on renal function
description: Zoledronic acid is associated with reports of renal impairment and renal failure, especially in patients with pre-existing renal dysfunction or other risk factors. Renal function should be measured before each dose, and patients should be adequately hydrated before treatment. Renal function monitoring is recommended after use of zoledronic acid in at-risk patients—especially those with pre-existing renal impairment. Use in patients with severe renal impairment is generally not recommended, but may be considered for tumour-induced hypercalcaemia, if the benefits outweigh the risks.

title: Emollients: new information about risk of severe and fatal burns with paraffin-containing and paraffin-free emollients

title: Nexplanon (etonogestrel) contraceptive implants: reports of device in vasculature and lung
description: There have been rare reports of Nexplanon implants having reached the lung via the pulmonary artery. An implant that cannot be palpated at its insertion site in the arm should be located as soon as possible and removed at the earliest opportunity. If an implant cannot be located within the arm, perform chest imaging. Correct subdermal insertion reduces the risk of these events.

title: Phenytoin: risk of Stevens-Johnson syndrome associated with HLA-B*1502 allele in patients of Thai or Han Chinese ethnic origin
description: If patients are known to be HLA-B*1502-positive, phenytoinshould be avoided when alternative therapy can be given.

title: Cilostazol (Pletal): risks of cardiovascular and bleeding events
description:
Indication restricted to second-line treatment and contraindicated with some cardiovascular conditions and medicines 

Furthermore, cilostazol is now contraindicated in patients with any of the following:


Unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction or coronary intervention (within 6 months)

a history of severe tachyarrhythmia

those receiving two or more other antiplatelet or anticoagulant treatments


For patients starting cilostazol, prescribers should assess benefit after 3 months of treatment, and should stop treatment if patients have not made clinically relevant improvements in walking distance. All patients who are currently receiving long-term treatment should be reassessed at a routine appointment, in light of the new advice.

title: Carfilzomib (Kyprolis▼): reminder of risk of potentially fatal cardiac events
description: Anti-cancer therapy with carfilzomib has been associated with cases of cardiac arrest, cardiac failure, and myocardial infarction, including in patients without pre-existing cardiac disorders. Monitor patients for signs and symptoms of cardiac disorders before and during exposure to carfilzomib.

Accord Healthcare Limited has informed us that there is an error on the portion of the Patient
Information Leaflet (PIL) for the Paracetamol Infusion which is intended for Healthcare Professionals.

description: Perform a quantitative serum anti-JCV antibody test—including index value—to support risk stratification for progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy. For high-risk patients, consider more frequent MRI screening.

description: Manufactured by Acutronic Medical Systems AG – Ventilator may switch off without user input, deliver incorrect ventilation or may fail to alarm.

description: Crescent Pharma Ltd has informed us that the Patient Information Leaflet (PIL) within the packs for the below batches are missing important safety relevant text changes.

title: Stop using Vaginal Speculums with smoke tube and Gynaecological Hysteroscopy sheaths from Gemini Surgical UK: all lots and batches. DSI/2022/001
description: The MHRA has become aware that Vaginal Speculums with smoke tube and Gynaecological Hysteroscopy sheaths from Gemini Surgical UK are being sold with a falsely applied CE mark. This means that these devices have been sold without evidence of safety and have been manufactured to unknown standards. They may also be labelled as Gemini Medical Innovations.

title: EyeCee One preloaded and EyeCee One Crystal preloaded intraocular lenses (IOLs): stop using immediately and quarantine all preloaded EyeCee One lenses DSI/2023/001
description: The MHRA is aware of reports of increased intraocular pressure in patients recently implanted with EyeCee One preloaded and EyeCee One Crystal preloaded intraocular lenses (IOLs).

title: SteriFeed Colostrum Collection device and risk of choking due to infant airway occlusion, DSI/2023/010
description: The SteriFeed colostrum collection device is intended to be used only to collect and store colostrum. It should not be used as an infant feeding device as the cap, if not removed, can easily get dislodged and become stuck in the baby’s throat.

title: Oral retinoids: pregnancy prevention—reminder of measures to minimise teratogenic risk
description: The risk of foetal malformation with oral retinoids is extremely high, even when used at a low dose or for a short time during pregnancy. All oral retinoids have an associated Pregnancy Prevention Programme (PPP), which is supported by educational material for prescribers, pharmacists, and patients. Women of child-bearing potential should have pregnancy excluded before starting treatment. While taking these medicines, one—or preferably two—different forms of contraception must be consistently used
